channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Enroll devices using a device enrollment manager account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device enrollment manager account to enroll devices in Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Wipe - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to wipe, or factory reset, devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Endpoint analytics overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics provides actionable insights to optimize device performance, improve user experience, and enable proactive IT troubleshooting.

item.title: Enable Windows diagnostic data and license verification - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enable Intune tenant settings to use Windows diagnostic data and verify Windows licenses required for dependent features.

item.title: Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect your Intune account to your Managed Google Play account.

item.title: Add corporate identifiers to Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add corporate identifiers (enrollment method, IMEI, and serial numbers) to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Query - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device query in Microsoft Intune to get on-demand device state, run Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries, and troubleshoot devices.

item.title: Restrict USB Devices Using Settings Catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to restrict USB devices, including thumb drives, flash drives, USB cameras, and more. Use other settings to allow specific USB devices on Windows 10/11 devices.

item.title: Set up enrollment for Android Enterprise fully managed devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment in Intune for devices using the Android Enterprise fully managed device management solution.

item.title: Configure Universal Print Policy Using Settings Catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure a Universal Print policy using the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to automatically install printers on managed Windows devices.

item.title: Configure Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step-by-step guide to configure endpoint analytics in Microsoft Intune, enroll devices, and manage data collection policies for device insights.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS direct enrollment - Apple Configurator-Setup Assistant - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Apple Configurator to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices with Setup Assistant.

item.title: Set up Intune enrollment for Android Enterprise dedicated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure enrollment in Microsoft Intune for Android Enterprise dedicated devices.

item.title: Device Action: Retire - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to retire device using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run KQL queries, analyze inventory trends, and start querying your managed fleet now.

item.title: Bulk enrollment for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a bulk enrollment package for Microsoft Intune

item.title: Device Action: Collect Diagnostics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to collect diagnostics with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Locate Device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Locate lost or stolen devices by using the locate device feature in Microsoft Intune. Get details on security and privacy information when using the locate device action.

item.title: Update Microsoft 365 Apps With the Settings Catalog in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to update Microsoft 365 apps to the latest version, and choose how frequently Office checks for updates. See the device registry keys that are updated when an Intune policy to Office update is applied.

item.title: Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, tvOS, visionOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available device actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up enrollment time grouping - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview and setup of the enrollment time grouping feature in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deploy Microsoft Edge policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create settings using the settings catalog to configure Microsoft Edge on Windows and macOS devices. Using Microsoft Intune, you can configure group policy settings, and deploy these settings to Microsoft Edge users.

item.title: Enable Windows Backup for Organizations - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enable Windows backup and restore in Intune for employees or students.

item.title: Set up Android Enterprise work profile for corporate owned devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Android Enterprise work profile for corporate-owned devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Fresh Start - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Fresh Start to remove or uninstall apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Upgrade the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway server software - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand how Microsoft Tunnel Gateway upgrades to new versions of the tunnel software for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Delete - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to delete devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up web based device enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up web-based device enrollment to manage user-owned iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Require multifactor authentication for Intune device enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to require multifactor authentication in Microsoft Entra ID for Intune device enrollment.

item.title: Device Action: Remove Apps and Configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how apps and configurations can be removed temporarily, then restored automatically or manually using the Remove apps and configuration device action with Intune.

item.title: Automatically Hide Devices With Cleanup Rules - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune's device cleanup rules offer a simple, automated way to ensure that only actively managed devices remain visible in the admin center. Learn more about device cleanup rules and how to configure them.

item.title: Device Action: Rename Device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Rename device action in Microsoft Intune to update the device name shown in the admin center. Useful for standardizing naming conventions, managing shared devices, and improving inventory clarity.

item.title: Create device platform restrictions - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Restrict personally owned devices, and specific device platforms and OS versions from enrolling in Intune.

item.title: Set up user enrollment with Company Portal for iOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up the profile based Apple User Enrollment option for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Remote Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the remote lock action in Microsoft Intune to lock a managed device that has a passcode or PIN.

item.title: Device Action: Reset Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to reset a passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up Android (AOSP) device management in Intune for corporate-owned userless devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned userless devices built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) platform.

item.title: Use direct enrollment for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy and enroll macOS devices in Microsoft Intune using direct enrollment with Apple Configurator.

item.title: Device Action: Sync - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the device sync action in Intune to apply policy, app, and configuration updates to managed devices.

item.title: Device Action: Send Custom Notification - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to create and send custom notifications to users of iOS/iPadOS and Android devices.

item.title: Set up Android (AOSP) device management in Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) platform.

item.title: Device Action: Lost Mode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enable Lost Mode in Microsoft Intune to remotely lock a lost or stolen iOS or iPadOS device and display a custom message and phone number on the lock screen.

item.title: Device Scopes - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device scopes in Microsoft Intune with scope tags for custom device reporting and targeted insights.

item.title: Set up enrollment notifications in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment notifications in Intune for employees or students.

item.title: Device Action: Autopilot Reset - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Autopilot reset with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up Apple device management in Intune for corporate-owned userless devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned userless iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Windows enrollment attestation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out how the Device attestation status report can help you and learn to use the Attest device action to ensure your devices are secure and reliable.

item.title: Create device limit restrictions - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Restrict the number of devices allowed to enroll in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device staging overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: An overview of device staging, Android Enterprise token types, and token management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: BitLocker Key Rotation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the BitLocker recovery key with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure ChromeOS connector for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect the Google Admin Console to Microsoft Intune so that you can view and take action on enrolled ChromeOS devices.

item.title: Device Action: Update Cellular Data Plan - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to update the cellular data plan for iOS/iPadOS devices that support eSIM with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Restart - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to restart managed devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Remove Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune remove passcode action helps you unlock devices remotely when users forget their passcodes. Discover how to use this feature.

item.title: Device Action: Run Remediation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to initiate on demand remediations with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Rotate Recovery Lock Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the macOS Recovery Lock passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Rotate local admin password - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the local admin password on Windows and macOS devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Education Windows device enrollment with provisioning packages - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to enroll Windows devices with provisioning packages using SUSPCs and Windows Configuration Designer.

item.title: Device Action: Logout Current User - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the logout the current user from a Shared iPad with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Move from Android device administrator to mobile application management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to set up mobile application management, an alternative option to Android device administrator that focuses on app protection and doesn't require device enrollment.

item.title: Device Action: Full Scan - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to initiate on demand Microsoft Defender full scan with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Apple School Manager Program enrollment for iOS/iPadOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to set up Microsoft Intune with Apple School Manager for corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Device Action: New Remote Assistance Session - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the new remote assistance session action in Intune to offer support to your users.

item.title: Device Action: Play Lost Mode Sound - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Play lost device sound action in Microsoft Intune to trigger an audible alert on a lost, stolen, or misplaced device—helping users locate it quickly and securely.

item.title: Device Action: Pause Config Refresh - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to temporarily pause policy enforcement on Windows 11 devices using Intune's Pause Config Refresh action to support troubleshooting and manual changes.

item.title: Device Action: Remove User - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to remove a user from a Shared iPad with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Shutdown - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to shutdown Apple devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Quick Scan - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to initiate on demand Microsoft Defender quick scan with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Disable Activation Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Microsoft Intune to disable Activation Lock on Apple devices.

item.title: Set up just-in-time registration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up JIT registration in Intune for devices enrolling via a supported Apple device enrollment or user enrollment method.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Macs into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Device Action: Rotate FileVault Recovery Key - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the FileVault recovery key with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for tvOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Apple TV devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Device Action: Deprovision - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to deprovision a chromeOS device with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Suspend Managed Home Screen - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to suspend the Managed Home Screen with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Enroll personal devices in Intune with Android Enterprise work profile management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for personal devices and bring-your-own-device scenarios using Android Enterprise work profile management.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for visionOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Apple Vision Pro devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Device Action: Restore Managed Home Screen - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to restore the Managed Home Screen with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Run remote actions, manage scripts and remediations, view device inventory, and export reports in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune documentation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Explore guides, tutorials, and references for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: What is Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune manages users and devices, simplifies app management and automated policy deployment, and integrates with mobile threat defense. It connects to Managed Google Play, Apple tokens and certificates, and Teamviewer for remote assistance. Can use MDM or MAM to protect data, configure devices, and simplify access to company resources.

item.title: Android device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android and Android Enterprise corporate-owned work profile, personally owned devices with a work profile, fully managed, AOSP, and dedicated devices in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Windows device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Windows devices using Automatic enrollment, Windows Autopilot, group policy, and co-management enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Use Remediations to Detect and Fix Support Issues - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about Remediations in Microsoft Intune, including what Remediations are and view any prerequisites and licensing requirements. Also, learn how to deploy built-in and custom remediation scripts, and learn how to monitor your scripts.

item.title: How to Configure the Intune Company Portal Apps, Company Portal Website, and Intune App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can configure the Intune Company Portal apps, Company Portal website, and Intune app.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune provides specific report types with focused views that contain consistent and timely data.

item.title: Linux device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Linux devices in Intune using the Intune app. Set an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Intune Graph API - Reports and Properties - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Intune reports and properties provided via Graph API.

item.title: Manage endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Security Administrators can use the Endpoint Security policies and profiles to focus on security configuration of devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device security - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage device compliance, security baselines, Microsoft Defender integration, VPN tunnels, identity protection, and threat defense with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Endpoint security in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage endpoint security in Microsoft Intune. Configure security policies, deploy baselines, and integrate with Defender for Endpoint to protect devices.

item.title: View report details for encryption status of devices managed with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to view reports for device encryption status across macOS FileVault and Windows BitLocker encrypted devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Intune for increased security - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to improve your security posture with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage devices with endpoint security in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Security Administrators can use the Endpoint Security node to view devices and manage them in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use Microsoft Tunnel VPN with Android devices that don't enroll with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add support for Mobile Application Management (MAM) for Android to the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. Tunnel support for MAM expands access to your organizational resources for devices that can't or haven't enrolled with Microsoft Intune
